description: Exploitation of weak authentication and session management mechanisms.

complexity_factors.question: Was the attack credential stuffing (using leaked real credentials) rather than simple brute force?
complexity_factors.reason: Credential stuffing uses real username/password pairs from previous breaches, making it much harder to distinguish from legitimate login attempts based on patterns alone.
complexity_factors.source: MITRE T1110.004 — Credential Stuffing: harder to detect than brute force due to low per-account attempt rate

complexity_factors.question: Did the attacker successfully take over at least one account?
complexity_factors.reason: A successful takeover means the blue team must now identify what the attacker did with the account, revoke access, and assess data exposure.
complexity_factors.source: OWASP A07:2021 — Authentication Failures: successful account takeover escalates from detection to full incident response

complexity_factors.question: Was a privileged or admin account the target of the attack?
complexity_factors.reason: Admin account compromise gives the attacker application-wide access, drastically expanding the scope of investigation and containment.
complexity_factors.source: MITRE T1078.003 — Local Accounts: privileged account compromise rated critical severity requiring immediate containment

complexity_factors.question: Was there no rate limiting or lockout policy in place?
complexity_factors.reason: Absence of rate limiting allowed the attack to proceed unimpeded, meaning the attack window was longer and more attempts were made.
complexity_factors.source: OWASP A07:2021 — missing rate limiting is an explicit authentication weakness that increases attack success probability

ttc_ranges.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.3: Low severity incidents should be contained rapidly with minimal escalation
NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.3: Medium severity incidents require analysis before containment
NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.3: High severity incidents involve multiple IR phases before containment is possible

rules.description: Alert must be investigated within 10 minutes of being raised
rules.trigger: No alert_investigation_started event within 600 seconds of alert_raised
rules.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.2.4: Initial analysis should begin promptly after detection

rules.description: Incident must be confirmed — authentication attack must be escalated
rules.trigger: No incident_confirmed event in event log
rules.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.2: Incident declaration is a required step in the IR lifecycle

rules.description: Containment must be initiated before max_tolerance threshold
rules.trigger: No containment_initiated event before max_tolerance_min seconds
rules.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.3: Account lockout or session revocation must be applied promptly

rules.description: Containment must succeed — account locked and session revoked
rules.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.3: Containment must be confirmed before incident can be closed

scenarios.label: Brute force on standard account — good response
scenarios.description: Blue team detects repeated login failures, confirms brute force, locks account and revokes sessions within acceptable time.

logs.detail: Wazuh rule 5712 fired — multiple authentication failures from single source IP
Analyst-1 opened alert and reviewed authentication logs
Analyst-1 confirmed brute force pattern across 200+ failed attempts
Analyst-2 initiated IP block and account lockout
Source IP blocked, targeted account locked

scenarios.label: Credential stuffing on admin — account taken over
scenarios.description: Credential stuffing bypasses detection initially. Admin account taken over before containment. Needs review verdict.

logs.detail: Wazuh rule 2503 fired — successful login after multiple failures from external IP
Analyst-2 confirmed admin account taken over via credential stuffing
Analyst-3 initiated forced session termination and password reset
Admin session terminated, password reset forced — but takeover already occurred
